perturbation.original_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein; but none of them is responsible for the completeness or accuracy of the information concerning the other persons making the filing, unless such person knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein. Each party is fully responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning all persons making the filing, regardless of their knowledge or belief about its accuracy.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that each party is only responsible for their own information's accuracy, unless they know the other's information is inaccurate. The modified text contradicts this by making each party fully responsible for the accuracy of all information, removing the 'knowledge' contingency. This creates uncertainty because it's unclear if a party can rely on others' due diligence or is ultimately liable for any errors, irrespective of their awareness.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Pursuant to and in accordance with the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, and the rules and regulations thereunder (the "Exchange Act") the undersigned hereby agree to the joint filing on behalf of each of them of any filing required by such party under Section 13 of the Exchange Act or any rule or regulation thereunder (including any amendment, restatement, supplement, and/or exhibit thereto) with respect to securities of Tallgrass Energy, LP, a Delaware limited partnership, and further agree to the filing, furnishing, and/or incorporation by reference of this Agreement as an exhibit thereto. Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein. Each party is fully responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning all persons making the filing, regardless of their knowledge or belief about its accuracy. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the earlier part of the same text. The original text states that each party is only responsible for their own information unless they knew or had reason to believe the others' information was inaccurate, while the changed text makes each party fully responsible for the accuracy of all information regardless of their knowledge, leading to a strong in-text contradiction that affects the legal understanding of the parties' responsibilities.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect until revoked by any party hereto in a signed writing provided to each other party hereto, and then only with respect to such revoking party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect unless terminated by mutual written consent of all parties, or unless automatically terminated after one year from the date of execution, regardless of any revocation by individual parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows individual parties to revoke the agreement with written notice, but only for themselves. The modified text adds two contradictory conditions for termination: mutual written consent of all parties (which requires unanimous agreement, unlike individual revocation), and automatic termination after one year. This creates ambiguity about how and when the agreement can be terminated. The original states one way, while the perturbation creates new ways to terminate the agreement that are in opposition with the previous statement in the original.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein. Each party is fully responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning all persons making the filing, regardless of their knowledge or belief about its accuracy. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect unless terminated by mutual written consent of all parties, or unless automatically terminated after one year from the date of execution, regardless of any revocation by individual parties. This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces new termination conditions (mutual consent and automatic termination after one year) that directly conflict with the original text's provision allowing individual parties to revoke the agreement. The contradicted text, which contains the changed text, now states that the agreement remains in effect unless terminated by mutual consent, automatically after a year, or revoked individually. The original text only specified individual revocation. The added conditions contradict the initial revocation clause, creating an in-text contradiction as the individual revocation allowance is made ineffective if mutual consent is not granted, or unnecessary if a year has passed. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition as the clause is now placed in a way that creates conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement must be executed in a single, original document to be considered valid. Counterparts are strictly prohibited and will render the agreement null and void.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly allows the agreement to be executed in multiple counterparts, which are then considered a single instrument. The modified clause directly contradicts this by stating that the agreement must be executed in a single original document and that counterparts are prohibited and invalidate the agreement. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the acceptable form of execution of the agreement. It creates two totally opposed statement that create an in-text contradiction that can have uncertanty about the legal consequences if the agreement is executed with counterparts or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of them is responsible for the timely filing of such filings and any amendments thereto, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning such person contained therein. Each party is fully responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning all persons making the filing, regardless of their knowledge or belief about its accuracy. This Agreement shall remain in full force and effect unless terminated by mutual written consent of all parties, or unless automatically terminated after one year from the date of execution, regardless of any revocation by individual parties. This Agreement must be executed in a single, original document to be considered valid. Counterparts are strictly prohibited and will render the agreement null and void.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself within the Contradicted Text section. The last two sentences in the 'Contradicted Text' section state: 'This Agreement must be executed in a single, original document to be considered valid. Counterparts are strictly prohibited and will render the agreement null and void.' This statement is in direct opposition to the original clause that allowed for execution in counterparts, thus creating a clear in-text contradiction regarding the valid execution method of the agreement.
